forest_walk_T063.bob_head: CameraRail bob-head is correctly implemented: 2Hz sinusoidal Y offset with amplitude 0.04 world units. This gives the player a walking gait feel — subtle enough not to cause motion sickness, pronounced enough to convey locomotion. elapsedMoving only advances when not paused, so the bob phase stays coherent across pause/resume cycles.
forest_walk_T063.no_free_camera: OrbitControls entirely absent from CameraRail and CoastBiome files. Player has zero camera control — the rail is fully automatic. Combined with CatmullRom spline and speed=0.008, the traversal feels authored and cinematic rather than exploratory.
forest_walk_T063.lookAhead: lookOffset=(0, 0.3, 0) slightly tilts gaze upward — natural for walking through environment, avoids staring at floor.
forest_walk_T063.score: 8.5/10 — solid rail walker implementation. Improvement opportunity: add slight horizontal sway (lookAhead X oscillation at 0.5Hz) for more organic feel in future cycle.

lair_scene_T076.density_assessment: The three additional shelves on the left wall fill what was previously empty stone. At x=-11.4, they create visual depth and frame the space — the eye now travels left and finds content (books) rather than bare wall. The staggered Y and Z positions avoid a uniform grid feel.
lair_scene_T076.moonlight_shaft: SpotLight 0xb8d4ff (cool blue-white) at angle PI/9 creates a narrow shaft from the top-left. Against the warm orange candlelight (0xff6622), this color contrast is atmospheric and evocative of Celtic moonlight through a stone window. Intensity 1.8 is strong — may wash out if candle intensity is low, but acceptable for visual impact.
lair_scene_T076.parchment_rolls: Three CylinderGeometry rolls on the floor (y=-4.8) with varied rotation.y add narrative clutter. Small (r=0.07) but convincing as scattered scrolls. Color 0xd4b878 (warm parchment) reads well against the dark flagstone floor.
lair_scene_T076.overall_lair_score: 7.5/10 — room feels more inhabited. Next improvement: add a flickering light source near the window to animate the moonlight shaft (currently static SpotLight).

mobile_T059.assessment: The min() CSS function is the correct approach for timer bars — they'll shrink gracefully on 375px without breaking layout. The max-width:100% on canvas prevents Three.js/canvas overflow. The @media 390px title font reduction is conservative (14px from likely 16-18px base) and appropriate.
mobile_T059.risk: LOW — canvases that size themselves via game logic (width=gridCols*tileSize etc.) may still set pixel widths on the canvas element itself. The CSS max-width constrains the display size but the canvas buffer resolution stays at its pixel dimensions — correct behavior for HiDPI-aware rendering.

analysis.overall_assessment: Cycle 29 is a verification and density cycle: T063 confirms the forest walk system was already complete (no regressions from previous cycles), T059 addresses the last mobile overflow edge cases, and T076 adds meaningful visual density to the lair. The moonlight shaft is the standout addition — it creates a strong second light source that contrasts beautifully with the candlelight. The game continues to feel more complete and atmospheric with each cycle.
